sourceMovie.title: The Raid 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Raid 2 (2014) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Transporter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Transporter shares The Raid 2's gift for action — the 2002 film delivers crime without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Raid
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes The Raid 2 compelling — the action, the crime — shows up in The Raid (2012) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: The Equalizer 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action energy of The Raid 2 is alive in The Equalizer 3 (2023), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Shoot 'Em Up
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Shoot 'Em Up (2007) operates in the same action as The Raid 2, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Dragged Across Concrete
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Dragged Across Concrete (2019) belongs to the same conversation as The Raid 2 — both anchor their action in crime emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Night Comes for Us
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Raid 2's action stayed with you, The Night Comes for Us (2018) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The crime through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Avengement
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Raid 2 excels in action, Avengement (2019) doubles down on crime to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Ninja: Shadow of a Tear
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ninja: Shadow of a Tear (2013) is the natural next step after The Raid 2: same action DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and crime elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Unleashed
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Raid 2 who respond to its action will find Unleashed (2005) immediately familiar. Both films share a crime sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Black Rain
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Raid 2 fans keep landing on Black Rain (1989) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Raid 2?
faq.answer: Try The Transporter, The Raid, The Equalizer 3—each shares DNA with The Raid 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Raid 2?
faq.answer: The Raid 2 (2014) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Raid 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Raid Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
